relationship_corrections.reason: An actor (user) has exactly one user profile, not many-to-many.
One actor (user) should be associated with exactly one user profile, not zero or many.
A dropdown contains many reasons, not the other way around.
A group should own multiple resources, not many groups owning a single resource.
Each post is owned by exactly one user, not many-to-many.
A user can have many resources, but a resource is owned by one user, so many-to-one is reversed.
A user account is associated with exactly one actor (user), not a composition with many actors.
Same as above: one user account links to one actor, and composition cardinality is incorrect.

architecture_corrections.description: Task #177 (Admin Verified Badge Grant/Revoke) owns a VerifiedBadge class, but it does not exist in the domain model. A VerifiedBadge class should be added and associated with User.
Task #164 (Follow/Unfollow Users with Counts) owns a Follow class, but it is missing. A Follow class should be added, distinct from Friendship (two-way).
Both User and UserAccount exist, with similar roles. UserAccount appears to be a technical replica; all task descriptions refer to User. They should be merged into a single User class.
BlockRecord is named inconsistently with task #175, which calls it 'Block'. The class should be renamed to Block for ubiquitous language alignment.
NotificationPreference has an incorrect association with Post. Preferences are per category (likes, comments, etc.), not per post. The association with Post should be removed.
REQ_REG_01, REQ_SEARCH_01, REQ_PRIV_MES_01, REQ_MOD_01, REQ_MEM_01 are requirement artifacts, not domain entities. They clutter the model and should be removed or moved to a separate context.

architecture_suggestions.suggestion: Rename BlockRecord to Block to match ubiquitous language from Task #175.
UserAccount appears to be a duplicate of User. Consider merging to avoid confusion and maintain a single user entity.
Requirement classes are not domain entities; they should be removed from the domain model to keep it focused on business concepts.
Consider introducing a value object for post content (e.g., PostContent) to encapsulate text and optional images, improving cohesion.
Operation is very generic and has many relationships. It might be better removed or broken into specific domain operations (e.g., SendMessageOp, RegisterUserOp).
